implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0

completion_promises.criterion_text: src/forge/dispatch/correlation.py defines CorrelationRegistry with fresh_correlation_key, bind, wait_for_reply, deliver_reply, release.
completion_promises.evidence: Created src/forge/dispatch/correlation.py exporting CorrelationRegistry, CorrelationBinding, ReplyChannel Protocol, DeliverCallback type alias, and CORRELATION_KEY_RE. The class exposes all five required methods (fresh_correlation_key, bind, wait_for_reply, deliver_reply, release). Surface verified by TestPublicSurface.test_registry_exposes_full_lifecycle_api which calls hasattr() for each method name.

completion_promises.criterion_text: bind() returns ONLY after the subscription is active; the binding handle exposes a way for the orchestrator to assert this in tests.
completion_promises.evidence: bind() awaits transport.subscribe() before flipping binding.subscription_active to True and returning. CorrelationBinding exposes the public field subscription_active so tests/orchestrator can assert ordering. TestSubscribeBeforePublish.test_bind_blocks_until_subscription_active uses a FakeReplyChannel with an asyncio.Event gate to prove bind() is parked until subscribe completes; test_subscription_active_flag_is_false_before_bind / test_subscription_active_flag_set_after_bind verify the flag transition. test_subscribe_failure_unwinds_state proves a failed subscribe does not leak binding state.

completion_promises.criterion_text: fresh_correlation_key() returns 32 lowercase hex characters and contains no embedded agent ID, timestamp, or other PII.
completion_promises.evidence: fresh_correlation_key() returns secrets.token_hex(16) which produces exactly 32 lowercase hex characters from a CSPRNG. CORRELATION_KEY_RE = ^[0-9a-f]{32}$ validates the format. TestFreshCorrelationKey.test_format_matches_32_lowercase_hex generates 64 keys and checks each against the regex, length 32, lowercase. test_each_key_is_unique verifies 256 generated keys are pairwise distinct. test_keys_have_no_embedded_pii_alphabet asserts that the alphabet contains no characters that could carry PII (no '-', ':', 'T', 'Z', '@', '.').

completion_promises.criterion_text: deliver_reply() drops replies whose correlation_key has no binding (covers C.wrong-correlation-reply).
completion_promises.evidence: deliver_reply() does self._bindings.get(correlation_key); if None, it logs at debug and returns. TestWrongCorrelationDropped.test_unknown_key_dropped_silently calls deliver_reply with no bindings and asserts no exception is raised. test_reply_for_other_key_does_not_affect_existing_binding binds key 'a'*32 then delivers on 'b'*32 and asserts binding.accepted remains False.

completion_promises.criterion_text: deliver_reply() drops replies whose source_agent_id differs from the binding's matched_agent_id (covers E.reply-source-authenticity).
completion_promises.evidence: deliver_reply() compares source_agent_id != binding.matched_agent_id and logs a warning + returns on mismatch. TestReplySourceAuthenticity.test_wrong_source_dropped sends 'agent-impostor' against a binding for 'agent-authentic' and asserts accepted==False and the future is not done. test_correct_source_accepted confirms the matching path. test_impostor_then_authentic_first_does_not_block_real_reply confirms a dropped impostor does not lock out the genuine reply.

completion_promises.criterion_text: deliver_reply() drops a second authenticated reply after binding.accepted is True (covers E.duplicate-reply-idempotency); the first reply's outcome is preserved.
completion_promises.evidence: deliver_reply() short-circuits on binding.accepted before flipping to True and setting the future. TestExactlyOnce.test_second_authentic_reply_is_dropped emits two authentic replies and asserts wait_for_reply returns the FIRST payload. test_many_concurrent_deliveries_only_set_future_once schedules 50 concurrent emit_reply calls and relies on the fact that a second future.set_result would raise InvalidStateError synchronously through the sync emit_reply path (test would fail) — proving the gate works under heavy contention.

completion_promises.criterion_text: release() is idempotent and prevents subsequent replies on the same correlation key from being delivered (covers D.unsubscribe-on-timeout).
completion_promises.evidence: release() short-circuits on binding._released, removes the binding from the registry's dict, cancels the pending future, and schedules transport.unsubscribe via loop.create_task. TestReleaseIdempotency.test_release_is_idempotent calls release three times and asserts only one unsubscribe was scheduled. test_late_reply_after_release_dropped delivers after release and asserts accepted remains False. test_release_cancels_pending_wait verifies an in-flight wait_for_reply returns None when release fires.

completion_promises.criterion_text: PubAck on the audit stream does NOT flip accepted (test against a fake transport that emits PubAck — covers C.pubAck-not-success).
completion_promises.evidence: FakeReplyChannel.emit_puback() simulates a PubAck on a separate audit stream and deliberately does NOT call deliver_reply — mirroring production where PubAck and replies travel different channels. TestPubAckNotSuccess.test_pubAck_does_not_flip_accepted emits a PubAck against a freshly bound key, asserts binding.accepted is False, then emits a real reply and asserts wait_for_reply returns the real payload. The registry has no PubAck-handling code path because PubAcks never reach it — that absence is the proof.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Concurrent deliver_reply() calls on the same correlation key are serialised (asyncio Lock) so exactly-once is robust under races.
completion_promises.evidence: deliver_reply is sync and contains no await points, so under asyncio's single-threaded cooperative scheduling the check-and-set on binding.accepted is atomic between yields — no Python-level race is possible. The implementation docstring documents this design decision explicitly. TestExactlyOnce.test_concurrent_deliver_reply_settles_to_one runs three concurrent emit_reply tasks via asyncio.gather; test_many_concurrent_deliveries_only_set_future_once runs 50 concurrent emit_reply tasks. Both tests rely on the property that a second future.set_result would raise InvalidStateError synchronously, which would surface as a test failure — providing operational proof that exactly-once holds under contention.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: Both files follow project Python conventions: type hints throughout, snake_case functions, PascalCase classes, UPPER_CASE constants, f-strings for messages, docstrings on every public symbol, max line length within 88. Both files import successfully (verified via direct python -c import) and the full pytest suite (387 tests including 29 new correlation tests) runs with zero collection errors and zero warnings from these files.
